title: Fix out-of-bounds access in lazy materialization with projection PREWHERE
body:
Fix out-of-bounds access in `getRequiredHeaderPositions` during `optimizeLazyMaterialization2` when a projection with PREWHERE is used.

When a projection adds `_projection_filter` to the expression DAG, `dag.getOutputs().size()` can exceed `required_output_positions.size()` (which is sized from the sorting step's output header). The loop at line 79 indexes into the `vector<bool>` without bounds checking, causing an abort in debug/sanitizer builds.

The fix caps the loop at `min(dag.getOutputs().size(), required_output_positions.size())`, so extra DAG outputs beyond the sorting header are safely ignored.

title: Fix flaky test_server_reload/test_change_listen_host under coverage
body:
Increase the timeout in `wait_loaded_config_changed` from 10 to 60 seconds.

The ZK-based config reload path relies entirely on ZK watches — the periodic 2-second file check does not cover ZK-only changes. Under coverage builds, the end-to-end latency (ZK watch delivery, background thread scheduling, config reload with socket rebinding) can exceed 10 seconds, causing the test to flake.

The polling loop exits immediately once the counter changes, so the increased timeout has no impact on the happy path.

CI report: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=101092&sha=246d165cac385dac5d10b74e71cddf422fec5a5e&name_0=PR&name_1=Integration%20tests%20%28amd_llvm_coverage%2C%205%2F5%29

title: Fix flaky test_polymorphic_parts repeated run failures
body:
### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)

### Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes to CHANGELOG.md):
- Not required

### Description

The `test_polymorphic_parts` integration test suite is chronically flaky — 21 distinct PRs affected in 30 days, with 3 master failures. The failures appear primarily in the CI `targeted` job (which uses `--count 10` via pytest-repeat) and in sanitizer builds (`amd_asan_ubsan, amd_asan`).

**Root causes identified:**

1. **Data accumulates across repeated test runs.** The `start_cluster` fixture is module-scoped: it creates tables once, but test functions run N times with `--count N`. Without cleanup between iterations, row counts grow (e.g., 4000 → 8000 → 12000) and assertions like `count() == '4000\n'` fail. In sanitizer CI builds, the accumulated data also amplifies replication load, causing `SYSTEM SYNC REPLICA` to timeout.

2. **`SYSTEM SYNC REPLICA` timeout of 20s is too tight for sanitizer builds.** ASAN/UBSAN builds run 2-5x slower. With 40 parts to replicate (20 INSERTs × 2 nodes), 20 seconds is insufficient even on a single run.

3. **`ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN` and `CREATE DATABASE` fail on repeated runs** without idempotent syntax.

**Fix:**
- Add `TRUNCATE TABLE` at the start of each test function to ensure a clean state
- Increase `SYSTEM SYNC REPLICA` timeout from 20s to 60s (3x margin for sanitizer overhead)
- Use `IF NOT EXISTS` for `CREATE DATABASE` and `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`
- Use `DROP TABLE IF EXISTS` before `CREATE TABLE` for non-replicated tables

**Verification:**
- Without fix: `test_compact_parts_only --count 3` → 2/3 fail (`assert '8000\n' == '4000\n'`)
- With fix: `test_compact_parts_only --count 5` → 5/5 pass
- Full suite single run: 7/7 pass

**Note:** A separate pre-existing ValuesBlockInputFormat parsing bug (306 CIDB hits in 30 days, `Cannot parse expression of type Array(Int32)`) also affects this test intermittently. That is a server-side issue affecting many integration tests, not specific to test_polymorphic_parts, and is not addressed here.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: groeneai

commits.message:
Fix flaky test_polymorphic_parts repeated run failures

The test_polymorphic_parts integration test suite fails when run with
pytest-repeat (--count N > 1), as used by the CI 'targeted' job:

1. Tables accumulate data across repetitions because the module-scoped
   fixture creates tables once but test functions run N times without
   cleanup. On iteration 2+, row counts don't match expected values.

2. SYSTEM SYNC REPLICA timeout of 20s is too tight for sanitizer builds
   (ASAN/UBSAN), which run 2-5x slower.

Fix:
- Add TRUNCATE TABLE at the start of each test function to ensure clean
  state between repetitions
- Increase SYSTEM SYNC REPLICA timeout from 20s to 60s
- Use IF NOT EXISTS for repeated CREATE operations
- Use ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for idempotent schema changes

title: Check for query cancellation in h3PolygonToCells
body:
The `h3PolygonToCells` function processes all rows in a tight loop calling H3 library functions (`maxPolygonToCellsSize` + `polygonToCells`) without ever checking if the query has been cancelled. Under MSan, these calls are extremely slow (~10-50x), and a query on 10,000 rows can run for ~30 minutes, ignoring `max_execution_time` and triggering the hung check in stress tests.

Add a per-row check of `QueryStatus::isKilled` (a single `std::atomic<bool>` load — essentially free) so the function respects cancellation.

CI report: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=100277&sha=53fa68604439a19ba43fbb7a772fb0f95a520e69&name_0=PR&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28amd_msan%29

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
The `h3PolygonToCells` function now respects query cancellation (`max_execution_time`, `KILL QUERY`), preventing it from running indefinitely on large inputs.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)

Author's comment: I'd not consider this approach, but the function is extremely slow, and its code is heavyweight, so this is normal.
author: alexey-milovidov

reviews.body: This will fix "hung check" in stress tests.

commits.message:
Check for query cancellation in `h3PolygonToCells` to avoid hung check in stress tests

The function processes all rows in a tight loop calling H3 library functions
(`maxPolygonToCellsSize` + `polygonToCells`) without ever checking if the
query has been cancelled. Under MSan, these calls are extremely slow, and
a query like `SELECT DISTINCT h3PolygonToCells(ring, 7) FROM rings` on
10,000 rows can run for ~30 minutes, ignoring `max_execution_time` and
triggering the hung check in stress tests.

Add a per-row check of `QueryStatus::isKilled` (a single atomic bool load)
so the function respects cancellation.

https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=100277&sha=53fa68604439a19ba43fbb7a772fb0f95a520e69&name_0=PR&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28amd_msan%29

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Fix "Invalid action query tree node" exception for UNION/INTERSECT with constant folding
body:
When a constant is folded from a `UNION` node (e.g. in queries using `INTERSECT ALL`), `calculateActionNodeName` cannot handle the `UNION` node type and throws a logical error. The existing check only excluded `QUERY` nodes from the source expression path but missed `UNION` nodes.

Found by the AST fuzzer in the stress test (TSan): https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=5a724175193032bb7178cecd7f5ccbff37319f7e&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=Stress+test+%28amd_tsan%29

### Changelog category (leave one):
- Bug Fix (user-visible misbehavior in an official stable release)

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Fix logical error "Invalid action query tree node" when using `INTERSECT ALL` / `UNION ALL` with constant-folded expressions.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: alexey-milovidov

reviews.body: This is a good change.

commits.message:
Fix "Invalid action query tree node" exception for UNION/INTERSECT with constant folding

When a constant is folded from a `UNION` node (e.g. in queries using
`INTERSECT ALL`), `calculateActionNodeName` cannot handle the `UNION`
node type and throws a logical error. The existing check only excluded
`QUERY` nodes from the source expression path but missed `UNION` nodes.

This was found by the AST fuzzer in the stress test (TSan) with query:
`SELECT min(*) FROM (SELECT number FROM numbers(10)) INTERSECT ALL SELECT min(*) FROM (SELECT number FROM numbers(10))`

https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=5a724175193032bb7178cecd7f5ccbff37319f7e&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=Stress+test+%28amd_tsan%29

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bugfix validation: use `remote` to trigger SECONDARY_QUERY context

The test did not reproduce the bug on a debug build because the
affected code path in `calculateActionNodeName` is only reached when
`isASTLevelOptimizationAllowed` returns false (SECONDARY_QUERY context).
Add a query using `remote` to force this context, similar to
03825_exists_constant_folded_action_name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bugfix validation: put INTERSECT ALL in SELECT list for `remote`

The previous test put the INTERSECT ALL scalar subquery in the WHERE
clause, where it gets evaluated on the initiator before the query is
sent to the remote node. This means the remote never sees the UNION
source expression on the ConstantNode, so the bug is not triggered.

Move the INTERSECT ALL expression into the SELECT list of the `remote`
query, matching the pattern used in `03825_exists_constant_folded_action_name`.
This ensures the ConstantNode with UNION source expression is part of
the query tree sent to the remote, where `isASTLevelOptimizationAllowed`
returns false and the affected code path in `calculateActionNodeName`
is actually exercised.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rename test to 04064 to avoid number conflict, update comments

The test number 04063 is already taken by another test on master.
Updated comments to explain that scalar subqueries are pre-evaluated
by the initiator and serialized as `_CAST`, so the UNION source
expression is not preserved when sent to remote nodes via `remote`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add proper bug reproducer using a view for UNION constant folding test

The previous test queries did not actually trigger the bug because scalar
subqueries are pre-evaluated by the initiator and serialized as `_CAST`,
losing the UNION source expression before reaching the shard.

The new test uses a view containing `INTERSECT ALL` as a scalar subquery,
queried via `remote()`. This forces view expansion on the shard in
`SECONDARY_QUERY` context where `isASTLevelOptimizationAllowed` returns
false, and the constant retains its UNION source expression — the exact
path that triggers the "Invalid action query tree node" exception.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use `--query_kind=secondary_query` to properly reproduce the bug in test

The previous test approaches (using `remote()` and views) did not actually
trigger the bug because the initiator pre-evaluates scalar subqueries before
sending them to shards, losing the UNION source expression.

Using `--query_kind=secondary_query` directly simulates a shard receiving
a distributed query, making `isASTLevelOptimizationAllowed` return false.
This causes the planner to try generating an action name from the UNION
source expression of the constant-folded INTERSECT ALL, which triggers
the "Invalid action query tree node" exception on unfixed builds.

Also renumber the test from 04064 to 04068 to avoid conflicts with tests
added to master.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: CI: remove experimental result file mechanism from runner
body:
The `experimental_file_name_static` / `experimental_from_fs` path in `Result` was designed to let a job write its result to a fixed `result_job.json` instead of the canonical `result_{job_name}.json`. No job in the codebase actually uses this path — it is dead code.

The runner code that checked for the experimental file after each job emitted a spurious warning on every run:

```
WARNING: no start_time set by the job - set job start_time/duration
```

Removing the entire mechanism eliminates the warning and simplifies both `runner.py` and `result.py`.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)
author: maxknv

commits.message:
CI: remove experimental result file mechanism from runner

The `experimental_file_name_static` / `experimental_from_fs` path in
`Result` allowed a job to write its result to a fixed `result_job.json`
file instead of the canonical `result_{job_name}.json`. No job in the
codebase actually uses this path, so it is dead code.

The runner code that checked for the experimental file after each job
emitted a spurious warning (`WARNING: no start_time set by the job -
set job start_time/duration`) on every run. Removing the entire
mechanism eliminates the warning and simplifies `runner.py` and
`result.py`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Add e2e integration tests for Iceberg and DataLake catalogs (running in private)
body:
Copies three end-to-end test suites and their helpers from the private repository into the public ClickHouse repo, so they can be run against real cloud backends (AWS Glue, OneLake, BigLake, Iceberg on S3/Azure).

## Tests added

- **`test_e2e_catalogs`** — `DataLakeCatalog` engine backed by AWS Glue, Microsoft OneLake, and Google BigLake
- **`test_e2e_iceberg_engine`** — `IcebergS3` and `IcebergAzure` table engines
- **`test_e2e_iceberg_tf`** — Iceberg table functions (S3 and Azure backends, including cluster queries)

## Infrastructure

New helper classes in `tests/integration/helpers/`:
- `catalog_manager.py` — abstract base class
- `catalog_manager_aws_glue.py`, `catalog_manager_biglake.py`, `catalog_manager_onelake.py` — catalog backends
- `catalog_manager_iceberg_s3.py`, `catalog_manager_iceberg_azure.py` — Iceberg storage backends

Supporting changes:
- `pytest.ini` — registers `e2e` and `only_backend` markers; excludes e2e tests from the default run (`-m 'not long_run and not e2e'`) so they don't run in standard CI
- `conftest.py` — loads `.env` via `python-dotenv` when present, so credentials can be supplied locally without modifying the environment
- `.env.example` — documents all required environment variables (`E2E_AWS_*`, `E2E_ICEBERG_AZURE_*`, `E2E_ONELAKE_*`, `E2E_BIGLAKE_*`)
- `.gitignore` — ignores `.env` and `tests/integration/.env` to prevent accidental credential commits

All credentials are read from environment variables at runtime; no secrets are hardcoded.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- Build/Testing/Packaging Improvement

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Added end-to-end integration tests for Iceberg table functions and engines (`IcebergS3`, `IcebergAzure`) and for the `DataLakeCatalog` engine backed by AWS Glue, Microsoft OneLake, and Google BigLake. These tests run against real cloud backends and are excluded from the default CI run (`-m 'not e2e'`).

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: fm4v

commits.message:
Add e2e integration tests for Iceberg and DataLake catalogs

Copies three test suites from the private repository:
- `test_e2e_catalogs` — DataLakeCatalog backends (AWS Glue, OneLake, BigLake)
- `test_e2e_iceberg_engine` — Iceberg Table Engine (`IcebergS3`/`IcebergAzure`)
- `test_e2e_iceberg_tf` — Iceberg Table Functions

Also adds the required helpers:
- `catalog_manager.py` and backend-specific implementations for AWS Glue,
  BigLake, OneLake, `IcebergS3`, and `IcebergAzure`

Supporting changes:
- `pytest.ini`: register `e2e` and `only_backend` markers; exclude `e2e` tests
  from the default run (`-m 'not long_run and not e2e'`)
- `conftest.py`: load `.env` via `python-dotenv` when present (credentials
  for e2e tests, never committed)
- `.env.example`: template documenting all required environment variables
- `.gitignore`: ignore `.env` and `/tests/integration/.env`

e2e tests: skip INSERT test only for biglake backend

Replace class-level \`@pytest.mark.xfail\` with a runtime \`pytest.xfail\`
inside \`test_insert_into_table\`, gated on the \`biglake\` backend, since
INSERT into BigLake DataLakeCatalog does not commit to the catalog.

title: Subtract slab_reclaimable from kernel memory in cgroupv2 reader
body:
The cgroupv2 memory usage calculation now uses `anon + sock + (kernel - slab_reclaimable)` instead of `anon + sock + kernel`. The `slab_reclaimable` portion is reclaimed synchronously by the kernel under memory pressure before invoking the OOM killer, so it should not count against the application's memory budget. This gives a more accurate picture of actual memory pressure.

On one of instances @azat saw that it can take significant portion of memory:

Process RSS: 8.47 GiB
anon: 7.75 GiB
kernel: 4.22 GiB (of which slab_reclaimable = 3.85 GiB)
MemoryTracking: 11.99 GiB (inflated ~42% vs real RSS)

The 3.85 GiB of reclaimable slab was dominated by ext4_inode_cache (with 56% usage only).

This inflated tracker causes premature `MEMORY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED` errors.

Cgroups v1 is not affected — its rss field excludes kernel memory.

Refs: https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/82036, https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/pull/83981

Also refactors `readMetricsFromStatFile` to populate a caller-owned map of individual metric values instead of returning a sum, allowing callers to apply their own aggregation logic.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- Improvement

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Cgroupv2 memory tracking now excludes `slab_reclaimable` from kernel memory, giving a more accurate measure of non-reclaimable memory usage.

cc @al13n321 as you added kernel for page cache.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: antonio2368

reviews.body: I'm OK with this version

Subtract `slab_reclaimable` from kernel memory in cgroupv2 reader

The cgroupv2 memory usage calculation now uses `anon + sock + (kernel - slab_reclaimable)`
instead of `anon + sock + kernel`. The `slab_reclaimable` portion is reclaimed synchronously
by the kernel under memory pressure before invoking the OOM killer, so it should not count
against the application's memory budget.

Also refactors `readMetricsFromStatFile` to populate a caller-owned map of individual metric
values instead of returning a sum, allowing callers to apply their own aggregation logic.

title: Transitive join predicates
body:
### Changelog category (leave one):
- Improvement


### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
The join order optimizer now infers transitive equi-join predicates from existing join conditions. For example, given `A.x = B.x AND B.x = C.x`, the equivalence `A.x = C.x` is recognized, allowing the optimizer to consider direct joins between transitively-connected tables. This can improve plan quality for star and snowflake schemas where dimension tables connect through a shared fact table. The feature is controlled by the new `enable_join_transitive_predicates` setting (off by default)

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)

title: Fix Iceberg `ALTER TABLE UPDATE` exception on partitioned tables.
body:
Fix Iceberg `ALTER TABLE UPDATE` exception on partitioned tables.

When a partitioned Iceberg table has had a prior `ALTER TABLE UPDATE`, the next UPDATE reads the original data file whose rows were removed by position deletes, producing a block with 0 rows. This empty block was passed to `ChunkPartitioner::partitionChunk`, where `buildScatterSelector` returned 0 partitions, triggering: `Logical error: 'partitions_count > 0'`.

Skip empty blocks in both the DELETE and UPDATE phases of `writeDataFiles` before passing them to the partitioner.

Closes https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/100565

### Changelog category (leave one):
- Bug Fix (user-visible misbehavior in an official stable release)


### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Fix `Logical error: 'partitions_count > 0'` exception when performing consecutive `ALTER TABLE UPDATE` on a partitioned Iceberg table.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: Desel72

reviews.body: Thanks, looks good

commits.message:
Fix Iceberg `ALTER TABLE UPDATE` exception on partitioned tables

When a partitioned Iceberg table has had a prior `ALTER TABLE UPDATE`,
the next UPDATE reads the original data file whose rows were removed by
position deletes, producing a block with 0 rows. This empty block was
passed to `ChunkPartitioner::partitionChunk`, where
`buildScatterSelector` returned 0 partitions, triggering:
Logical error: `partitions_count > 0`.

Skip empty blocks in both the DELETE and UPDATE phases of
`writeDataFiles` before passing them to the partitioner.

Closes https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/100565

title: Add missing Keeper component tracking guard to no-arg loadStatistics
body:
The no-arg `IMergeTreeDataPart::loadStatistics` overload was missing the
`Coordination::setCurrentComponent` guard. Its sibling overload
`loadStatistics(const Names &)` already had one.

When `getEstimates` calls the no-arg overload during query planning (via
`filterPartsByStatistics` in `ReadFromMergeTree::selectRangesToRead`), it
accesses Keeper metadata storage without a component set, causing a server
exception on configurations with "meta in keeper" + "s3 storage":

```
Logical error: Current component is empty, please set it for your scope
using Coordination::setCurrentComponent (STID: 2884-4370)
```


### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
...

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)
author: murphy-4o

commits.message:
Add missing `setCurrentComponent` guard to no-arg `loadStatistics` overload

The no-arg `IMergeTreeDataPart::loadStatistics` was missing the
Keeper component tracking guard, while its sibling overload
`loadStatistics(const Names &)` already had one. When `getEstimates`
calls the no-arg overload during query planning (via
`filterPartsByStatistics`), it accesses Keeper metadata storage
without a component set, triggering:

title: Fix flaky 01926_order_by_desc_limit test timeout under MSan + S3
body:
The test `01926_order_by_desc_limit` consistently times out (600s) in the MSan + distributed cache + S3 storage CI configuration. The root cause is the ~300MB data volume (307,200 rows × `repeat('a', 1024)`) being too expensive to INSERT, OPTIMIZE FINAL, and SELECT under MSan's instrumentation overhead combined with S3 network I/O.

Reduce the string size from 1024 to 128 bytes, cutting total data from ~300MB to ~40MB. The test's primary assertion — `read_rows <= 1024 * 110` in `query_log` — is unaffected since it checks row counts, not byte volumes. The `max_memory_usage = '400M'` guard becomes more generous but remains as a safety net.

Reduce the string size from 1024 to 128 bytes, cutting total data from

title: Fix logical error exception in lightweight updates with implicit_transaction
body:
## Summary

- Fix logical error exception "Storage does not support transaction. It is a bug" that occurs when running `UPDATE` (lightweight update) on a `ReplicatedMergeTree` table with `implicit_transaction = true` and `throw_on_unsupported_query_inside_transaction = false`
- The issue is in `MergeTreeDataWriter::writeTempPartImpl` which unconditionally uses the context's transaction TID when creating new parts via `MergedBlockOutputStream`, but `StorageReplicatedMergeTree` does not support transactions
- Added a `data.supportsTransactions()` check before using the transaction TID, falling back to `Tx::PrehistoricTID` for storages that don't support transactions

CI report: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=98353&sha=af58bfdd85c636501f2db757a7b3839c231f6e4a&name_0=PR&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28amd_msan%29

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Fix logical error exception in lightweight updates when `implicit_transaction` setting is enabled.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: alexey-milovidov

reviews.body: Ok.

commits.message:
Fix logical error exception in lightweight updates with `implicit_transaction`

When `implicit_transaction = true` and `throw_on_unsupported_query_inside_transaction = false`,
an UPDATE query on a ReplicatedMergeTree table causes a logical error exception
"Storage does not support transaction. It is a bug" because `writeTempPartImpl`
unconditionally uses the context's transaction TID when creating new parts.
Since `StorageReplicatedMergeTree` does not support transactions, the TID
should fall back to `Tx::PrehistoricTID`.

https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=98353&sha=af58bfdd85c636501f2db757a7b3839c231f6e4a&name_0=PR&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28amd_msan%29

title: CI: avoid OSError for long result filenames by not dumping sub-results
body:
When a job produces sub-results (tasks) whose names are derived from error
messages — for example the `adjustLastGranule` error from #100769 — the name
can exceed the 255-byte Linux filesystem limit. All setter methods
(`set_status`, `set_results`, `set_files`, `set_info`, `set_link`) on `Result`
unconditionally called `dump()`, which tried to open a JSON file named after
the result. This caused `OSError: [Errno 36] File name too long`, crashing the
CI harness entirely before it could upload or report any results.

Example failure: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=101315&sha=d65b7bf56cf5118ae84a6e2cf21759a9ad11bf7d&name_0=PR&name_1=Stateless+tests+%28amd_tsan%2C+s3+storage%2C+parallel%2C+1%2F2%29

**Fix:** sub-result files should never be created in the first place — only
job-level and workflow-level results need to be persisted to disk. A new
`_dump_if_persisted()` helper replaces the unconditional `self.dump()` in all
setters: it only writes to disk when a result file already exists. Sub-results
(tasks) are never explicitly dumped first, so they have no file on disk and the
setters are no-ops for them regardless of name length. Job-level results are
created explicitly by `complete_job`, `copy_result_to_s3`, or a direct
`dump()` call, and continue to be updated on every setter call as before. The
helper also catches `OSError` as a safety net.

cc @maxknv

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)
author: Algunenano

commits.message:
Truncate long result filenames to avoid OSError in CI

When a logical error message is very long (e.g. the `adjustLastGranule`
error), `Result.file_name_static` produces a filename exceeding the
255-byte Linux filesystem limit, causing `OSError: [Errno 36] File name
too long` and crashing the CI harness before it can report results.

Truncate the normalized name to fit within the limit, appending an MD5
hash suffix to preserve uniqueness.

https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=101315&sha=d65b7bf56cf5118ae84a6e2cf21759a9ad11bf7d&name_0=PR&name_1=Stateless+tests+%28amd_tsan%2C+s3+storage%2C+parallel%2C+1%2F2%29

Truncate by byte length, not character length

Address review: use UTF-8 encoded byte length for the truncation check
so that multibyte characters cannot exceed the 255-byte filesystem limit.

CI: fix OSError from long result filenames by not dumping sub-results

`set_status` and other setters on `Result` unconditionally called
`dump()`, which writes a JSON file named after the result. When a
sub-result (task) had a very long name — e.g. an error message from
`adjustLastGranule` — the generated filename exceeded the 255-byte Linux
filesystem limit, causing `OSError: [Errno 36] File name too long` and
crashing the CI harness before it could report any results.

Example: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=101315&sha=d65b7bf56cf5118ae84a6e2cf21759a9ad11bf7d&name_0=PR&name_1=Stateless+tests+%28amd_tsan%2C+s3+storage%2C+parallel%2C+1%2F2%29

Replace `self.dump()` in all setters with `_dump_if_persisted()`, which
only writes to disk when a result file already exists. Sub-results
(tasks) are never explicitly dumped first, so they get no file and the
setters become no-ops for them. Job-level results are created explicitly
by `complete_job`, `copy_result_to_s3`, or direct `dump()` calls and
are therefore updated normally.

Closes: ClickHouse/ClickHouse#101492

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Revert truncation of long result filenames

The truncation approach from commits 073e559ce29 and 0132933127e is
replaced by not creating result files for sub-results at all (see
previous commit). Sub-results with long names never call `dump()`, so
the filename length limit is not reached.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: only suppress ENAMETOOLONG in _dump_if_persisted, hard-fail on other errors

Re-raise any OSError other than ENAMETOOLONG so that real persistence
problems (permission denied, disk full, I/O error) remain visible
and fail the job loudly instead of leaving stale CI metadata.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Fix MATERIALIZE INDEX/PROJECTION mutation stuck after DROP
body:
## Summary

- Fix `MATERIALIZE INDEX` and `MATERIALIZE PROJECTION` mutations getting stuck with "Unknown index" / "No such projection" exceptions when the index or projection is dropped before the mutation finishes executing
- This can happen in replicated environments or after `KILL MUTATION` followed by `DROP INDEX`/`DROP PROJECTION`
- The fix makes `MutationsInterpreter::prepare` gracefully skip materialization when the target no longer exists, instead of throwing

Closes #38643

### Changelog category:
- Bug Fix (user-visible misbehavior in an official stable release)

### Changelog entry:
Fix `MATERIALIZE INDEX` and `MATERIALIZE PROJECTION` mutations getting stuck when the index or projection is dropped before the mutation finishes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: alexey-milovidov

reviews.body: This looks alright.

commits.message:
Fix MATERIALIZE INDEX/PROJECTION mutation stuck after index/projection is dropped

`MATERIALIZE INDEX` and `MATERIALIZE PROJECTION` mutations could get stuck
with "Unknown index" or "No such projection" exceptions if the index or
projection was dropped before the mutation finished executing. This can
happen in replicated environments or after `KILL MUTATION` followed by
`DROP INDEX`/`DROP PROJECTION`.

The fix makes `MutationsInterpreter::prepare` gracefully skip
materialization when the target index or projection no longer exists in
the metadata, instead of throwing an exception.

Closes #38643

### Changelog category:
- Bug Fix (user-visible misbehavior in an official stable release)

### Changelog entry:
Fix `MATERIALIZE INDEX` and `MATERIALIZE PROJECTION` mutations getting stuck when the index or projection is dropped before the mutation finishes.

title: Cancel WriteBuffer in IcebergStorageSink on query cancellation
body:
## Summary
- Fix logical error exception `"WriteBuffer is neither finalized nor canceled in destructor."` in `IcebergStorageSink` during stress tests
- When a query is cancelled, `onFinish` detected `isCancelled()` and returned immediately without finalizing or cancelling the write buffers. Since `onException` is not called on cancellation, the `WriteBuffer` reached its destructor in a bad state
- Fix by calling `cancelBuffers()` on the cancellation path in `onFinish`

CI reports:
- https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=d80ab8d9ab5feaf66b160ba6b626b7fb11d1c170&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28arm_asan%29
- https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=02494568f66fc9564136dcfd3dff2c4387caa178&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28arm_asan%2C%20s3%29

## Test plan
- [x] Verified `WriteBuffer::cancel` is safe to call on already-finalized buffers (returns immediately)
- [ ] Stress test (arm_asan) should pass without the logical error

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)

### Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
...

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk, minimal control-flow change in `IcebergStorageSink::onFinish` to ensure buffers are canceled on query cancellation, reducing destructor-time logical errors without changing normal write finalization behavior.
> 
> **Overview**
> Ensures `IcebergStorageSink::onFinish()` cancels in-flight write buffers when the query has been cancelled, instead of returning early and leaving buffers neither finalized nor canceled.
> 
> Normal completion behavior is unchanged: non-cancelled writes still `finalizeBuffers()` and `releaseBuffers()`.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 4e9e3dd5c94dd61a4d48d49b09adebdf2a7dfbd1. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: alexey-milovidov

commits.message:
Cancel `WriteBuffer` in `IcebergStorageSink` on query cancellation

When a query is cancelled during stress tests,
`IcebergStorageSink::onFinish` detected `isCancelled()` and returned
immediately without finalizing or cancelling the write buffers inside
`MultipleFileWriter`. Since `onException` is not called on
cancellation, the `WriteBuffer` reached its destructor in a state that
was neither finalized nor cancelled, triggering a logical error
exception.

Fix by calling `cancelBuffers()` on the cancellation path in
`onFinish`.

https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=d80ab8d9ab5feaf66b160ba6b626b7fb11d1c170&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28arm_asan%29
https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?REF=master&sha=02494568f66fc9564136dcfd3dff2c4387caa178&name_0=MasterCI&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28arm_asan%2C%20s3%29

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: CI: read GitHub App installation_id from AWS secret instead of fetching via API
body:
Previously `GHAuth` called `GET /app/installations` on every CI job authentication to discover the installation id, requiring an extra GitHub API round-trip each time.

The installation id is now stored in the existing `woolenwolf_gh_app` AWS secret (key: `installation_id`) and read directly from there.

## Changes

- Remove `_get_installation_id` from `GHAuth`; `installation_id` is now an explicit parameter to `auth`, `_get_access_token`, and `_get_access_token_deprecated`
- Add `SECRET_GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID` setting pointing to `woolenwolf_gh_app.installation_id`
- Update all callers (`runner.py`, `native_jobs.py`, `copilot_review_job.py`) to fetch all three secrets with a single `join_with` chain
- Add the new secret to `defs.py` and extend `validator.py` checks accordingly
- Implement `Secret.Config.get_aws_ssm_secrets_batched`: groups secret names by their root AWS secret and calls boto3 `get_secret_value` once per root — so `join_with` on secrets sharing the same AWS secret (all three `woolenwolf_gh_app.*` keys) now makes **one** API call instead of three

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)
author: maxknv

commits.message:
CI: read GitHub App installation_id from AWS secret instead of fetching via API

Previously `GHAuth` called `GET /app/installations` on every auth to
discover the installation id, which required an extra GitHub API round-trip
each time a CI job authenticates.

The installation id is now stored as `installation_id` in the existing
`woolenwolf_gh_app` AWS secret and is read directly from there.

Changes:
- Remove `_get_installation_id` from `GHAuth`; pass `installation_id` as a
  parameter to `auth`, `_get_access_token`, and `_get_access_token_deprecated`
- Add `SECRET_GH_APP_INSTALLATION_ID` setting (`woolenwolf_gh_app.installation_id`)
- Update all callers (`runner.py`, `native_jobs.py`, `copilot_review_job.py`) to
  fetch all three secrets with a single `join_with` chain
- Add the new secret to `defs.py` and extend `validator.py` checks
- Implement `get_aws_ssm_secrets_batched` in `Secret.Config`: groups names by
  their root secret and calls boto3 `get_secret_value` once per root, so
  `join_with` on secrets that share the same AWS secret (like all three
  `woolenwolf_gh_app.*` keys) now makes a single API call instead of one per key

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: fix get_aws_ssm_secrets_batched to use AWS CLI instead of boto3

boto3 raises NoRegionError in environments where the region is not exposed
as an environment variable (e.g. the copilot review job). The AWS CLI resolves
the region from all available sources the same way the existing
get_aws_ssm_secret does.

Replace the boto3 get_secret_value call with the same CLI command, but parse
the returned JSON SecretString in Python (json.loads) instead of piping through
jq. This handles multi-line values such as PEM keys correctly while keeping the
one-call-per-root batching benefit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: add `GHAuth.auth_from_settings` to remove duplication of secret fetching

Extract the repeated pattern of fetching `app_id`, `pem`, and
`installation_id` from AWS secrets into `GHAuth.auth_from_settings`, and
update `copilot_review_job.py` to use it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: replace manual secret fetching in GH auth with auth_from_settings

Now that GHAuth.auth_from_settings exists and reads all three secrets
via Settings, callers no longer need to fetch the secrets themselves.
The workflow parameter is removed from _GH_Auth in runner.py and
native_jobs.py since it was only needed for secret lookup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: fail copilot job on any exception; restore Secret import; add traceback

- Remove the broad exception catch in _run that silently skipped all errors
  (including programming errors like missing imports) as a warning
- Restore the 'from ci.praktika import Secret' import removed during refactor
- Print full traceback and store error message in Result.info on failure

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

CI: fix get_aws_ssm_secrets_batched for keyless secret requests

Calling json.loads unconditionally broke non-JSON secrets when the caller
requests a root secret without a key suffix (name has no dot). Only parse
the secret string as JSON when at least one entry in the group actually
needs a key; keyless entries return the raw string directly.

Fixes https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/pull/101194#discussion_r3015144285

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Fix ArrowFlight integration test port conflict under pytest-xdist
body: ## Problem The ArrowFlight docker compose file (`docker_compose_arrowflight.yml`) uses hardcoded host port mappings (`5005:5005` and `5006:5006`). When pytest-xdist runs multiple workers — as happens in targeted and flaky CI checks with `--dist=each` — each worker tries to start its own ArrowFlight container binding the same host ports, causing: ``` Error response from daemon: driver failed programming external connectivity on endpoint roottestarrowflightstorage-gw1-arrowflight1-1 (...): Bind for 0.0.0.0:5005 failed: port is already allocated ``` This has been causing persistent `test_arrowflight_storage` failures in PR #91170's targeted integration test check: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=91170&sha=1e7eaf8dc3ae5fb55097ee49170c1e5e3a83191b&name_0=PR&name_1=Integration%20tests%20%28amd_asan_ubsan%2C%20targeted%29 ## Root cause analysis Every other external service in the integration test framework (Mongo, Redis, Kafka, LDAP, etc.) uses dynamic port allocation from `PortPoolManager` to avoid host port conflicts between concurrent xdist workers. The ArrowFlight setup was missing this — `setup_arrowflight_cmd` did not allocate ports from the pool or set any port environment variables, and the compose file used literal port numbers. In **regular** (non-targeted) integration test batches, `test_arrowflight_storage` works fine because `--dist=loadfile` assigns each test module to a single worker, so only one ArrowFlight container ever exists at a time. The bug only manifests under `--dist=each` with multiple workers, which is the mode used by targeted and flaky checks. Once the test first failed in a targeted run (due to unlucky timing with port binding), it entered a self-perpetuating cycle: CIDB recorded the failure, which caused the targeted check to re-select `test_arrowflight_storage` on every subsequent commit, where it would fail again due to the port conflict, reinforcing the CIDB record. ## Fix Use dynamic port allocation from the port pool, following the same pattern as every other external service: - Added `arrowflight_port` and `arrowflight_auth_port` properties to `ClickHouseCluster` that allocate from `port_pool` - `setup_arrowflight_cmd` now sets `ARROWFLIGHT_EXTERNAL_PORT` and `ARROWFLIGHT_AUTH_EXTERNAL_PORT` environment variables - `docker_compose_arrowflight.yml` uses `${ARROWFLIGHT_EXTERNAL_PORT:-5005}:5005` and `${ARROWFLIGHT_AUTH_EXTERNAL_PORT:-5006}:5006` The internal container ports (5005/5006) remain unchanged. Tests connect via Docker networking (`arrowflight1:5005`) and are unaffected — only the host-side port mapping becomes dynamic. ### Changelog category (leave one): - CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required) ### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md): ... ### Documentation entry for user-facing changes - [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: yakov-olkhovskiy

commits.message:
Fix ArrowFlight integration test port conflict under pytest-xdist

The ArrowFlight docker compose file used hardcoded host port mappings
(5005:5005 and 5006:5006). When pytest-xdist runs multiple workers
(e.g. in targeted or flaky CI checks with --dist=each), each worker
starts its own ArrowFlight container trying to bind the same host
ports, causing 'port is already allocated' failures.

This was observed failing consistently in PR #91170's targeted
integration test check:
https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=91170&sha=1e7eaf8dc3ae5fb55097ee49170c1e5e3a83191b&name_0=PR&name_1=Integration%20tests%20%28amd_asan_ubsan%2C%20targeted%29

Fix: use dynamic port allocation from the port pool, following the
same pattern as every other external service (Mongo, Redis, Kafka,
etc.). The internal container ports (5005/5006) remain unchanged —
only the host-side mapping becomes dynamic. Tests connect via Docker
networking (`arrowflight1:5005`) and are unaffected.

Co-authored-by: Yakov Olkhovskiy <yakov-olkhovskiy@users.noreply.github.com>

title: Enforce both min and max block size thresholds during INSERT squashing
body:
### Changelog category (leave one):
- New Feature

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):

Implement new behavior of `max_insert_block_size_rows`, `max_insert_block_size_bytes`, `min_insert_block_size_rows`, `min_insert_block_size_bytes` in squashing under compatibility setting `use_strict_insert_block_limits`.

## Block Formation Logic

When use_strict_insert_block_limits is enabled, block emission during INSERT operations follows strict rules:

A block is emitted when either condition is satisfied:

Minimum thresholds met (AND logic): Both min_insert_block_size_rows AND min_insert_block_size_bytes are reached
Maximum threshold exceeded (OR logic): Either max_insert_block_size_rows OR max_insert_block_size_bytes is reached
This ensures blocks are:

Large enough (must meet both minimum criteria)
Not too large (capped by either maximum limit)
When disabled (default behavior):

Blocks emit when min_insert_block_size_rows OR min_insert_block_size_bytes is reached (OR logic)
Maximum limits are not enforced
Disabling individual settings: Set to 0 to exclude from block formation:

min_insert_block_size_rows = 0: only byte threshold matters
min_insert_block_size_bytes = 0: only row threshold matters
max_insert_block_size_bytes = 0: no byte maximum
max_insert_block_size_rows = 0: no row maximum

**Note**: If min settings are smaller than max settings, the max limits take precedence and blocks will be emitted before min thresholds are reached.

Continues https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/pull/92833, resolves https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/42515.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [x] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)


<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes core INSERT/materialized-view squashing and deduplication ordering, which can affect part formation and insert performance/behavior across multiple ingestion paths. Risk is mitigated by being gated behind the new `use_strict_insert_block_limits` setting and covered by new stateless/regression tests.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new compatibility setting `use_strict_insert_block_limits` and updates docs for `min_*`/`max_*_insert_block_size*` to distinguish **format parsing** vs **INSERT/materialized-view squashing** behavior.
> 
> Refactors `Squashing`/`PlanSquashingTransform`/`SquashingTransform` to support strict bound enforcement (AND for mins, OR for maxes) including partial-chunk slicing and accurate byte accounting (notably for `LowCardinality` dictionaries), and wires the new max-row/byte parameters through INSERT and materialized-view pipelines; strict mode is automatically disabled for async inserts by reordering deduplication token attachment.
> 
> Adds new stateless tests covering strict min/max splitting for `INSERT` and `INSERT…SELECT`, plus regressions for 0-row chunks and byte-underestimation causing `canGenerate()`/`generate()` mismatches.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 9a5fb08c9394afbf54f8ca786424317e8df6a6d2. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
author: Fgrtue

title: Fix endianness in `QBit` binary serialization
body:
`SerializationQBit` used native-endian `writeBinary`/`readBinary` for float elements in RowBinary format. On a big-endian platform this would produce corrupt data that cannot be read back by a little-endian node (or vice versa). All other numeric serializations use explicit little-endian encoding.

### Changelog category (leave one):

title: Fix UBSAN float-cast-overflow in ConditionSelectivityEstimator
body:
### Changelog category (leave one):
- Not for changelog (changelog entry is not required)

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Fix undefined behavior (UBSAN float-cast-overflow) in the statistics-based selectivity estimator when converting selectivity to row count.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)

---

## Summary

Fixes UBSAN `undefined-behavior` at `ConditionSelectivityEstimator.cpp:148` (`static_cast<UInt64>(final_rows)`), observed as STID 6239-5b30 across 4 unrelated PRs in CI (Stress test amd_asan_ubsan, Stateless tests amd_asan_ubsan).

**Root cause:** `ConditionSelectivityEstimator::ColumnEstimator::estimateRanges()` sums individual range estimates for each value in an `IN` clause. When there are many IN values relative to the table's row count, and the statistics (CountMin sketch) return non-zero estimates for each, the sum exceeds `getNumRows()`, producing a selectivity > 1.0. This unclamped selectivity flows through `finalize()`, and `selectivity * total_rows` can produce a Float64 value outside the valid UInt64 range. `static_cast<UInt64>()` on such a value is undefined behavior. Additionally, NaN can propagate if statistics produce floating-point edge cases.

**Fix (three defense layers):**
1. `estimateRanges()`: clamp return value to `[0, 1]` — selectivity cannot exceed 1.0 by definition
2. `finalize()`: clamp computed selectivity to `[0, 1]` with `std::isfinite()` NaN guard
3. `estimateRelationProfileImpl()`: check for NaN/Inf and clamp `final_rows` to `[0, total_rows]` before the `UInt64` cast
4. Initialize `RPNElement::selectivity = 0` (was uninitialized)

**Regression test:** `04075_statistics_selectivity_overflow` creates a table with `countmin` + `tdigest` statistics and runs compound AND queries with many IN values on a 100-row table, exercising the selectivity > 1.0 code path. The AND condition is required because the statistics estimator is only invoked during PREWHERE optimization when there are multiple conditions (`has_multiple_conditions` in `optimizePrewhere.cpp`).

**Note on changelog category:** Changed from "Bug Fix" to "Not for changelog" because this UBSAN undefined behavior is only detectable by sanitizer builds — the queries produce correct results on non-sanitizer builds. The Bugfix validation check cannot verify the fix on a regular build since the test output is identical with and without the fix; the actual validation relies on CI's ASAN+UBSAN builds producing zero UBSAN errors.

Verified: 20/20 passes with debug build + randomization.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: groeneai

commits.message:
Fix UBSAN float-cast-overflow in ConditionSelectivityEstimator

The selectivity estimator could produce undefined behavior when converting
a Float64 selectivity * total_rows to UInt64:

1. estimateRanges() sums individual range estimates (e.g. for IN with many
   values) that can exceed total rows, giving selectivity > 1.0
2. NaN can propagate through floating-point edge cases in statistics
3. The selectivity member was uninitialized in RPNElement

Fix:
- Initialize selectivity = 0 in RPNElement
- Clamp estimateRanges() return to [0, 1]
- Clamp finalize() selectivity to [0, 1] with NaN handling
- Add NaN/Inf/overflow guard before the UInt64 cast

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Extend float-to-string `itoa` fast path for large integer values
body:
Extend the `itoa` fast path in `writeFloatTextFastPath` for floating-point values that are integers but whose exponent exceeds the mantissa width (ULP > 1). In this range, multiple integers map to the same float, and shortest-representation algorithms like dragonbox prefer "rounder" decimals (more trailing zeros).

**What changed:**

1. For Float32 exp 24 (ULP=2) and Float64 exp 53 (ULP=2): all values are even integers, plain `itoa` matches dragonbox — added directly to the fast path.

2. For Float32 exp 25-30 (ULP 4..128): a rounding step finds the "roundest" integer within the valid dragonbox range by trying `% 1000`, `% 100`, `% 10` from largest to smallest.

3. For Float64 exp 54-62 (ULP 4..1024): same rounding approach, with an additional `% 10000` step for exp 62 (ULP=1024).

4. All modulo operations use compile-time constants (no hardware `div`). The IEEE 754 boundary conditions (power-of-2 asymmetry, even/odd mantissa tie-breaking) are handled correctly.

5. Negative values are handled by writing the sign first, then rounding the absolute value.

6. Added a performance test for Float64 large integers starting at 2^53.

**Range covered:**

| Type | Exponent range | Value range | ULP range |
|------|---------------|-------------|-----------|
| Float32 | exp 0..24 | integers up to ~16M | 1..2 (plain `itoa`) |
| Float32 | exp 25..30 | ~33M to ~2.1B | 4..128 (rounded `itoa`) |
| Float64 | exp 0..53 | integers up to ~9e15 | 1..2 (plain `itoa`) |
| Float64 | exp 54..62 | ~1.8e16 to ~9.2e18 | 4..1024 (rounded `itoa`) |

**Verification:**

- Float32: exhaustive check of all ~4.3 billion values (positive and negative) — zero mismatches with dragonbox.
- Float64: 10 billion random samples (500M per exponent, exp 53-62, both positive and negative) plus all power-of-2 boundary values — zero mismatches with dragonbox.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- Performance Improvement

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Faster float-to-string conversion for large integer values by extending the `itoa` fast path with dragonbox-compatible rounding.

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: Algunenano

reviews.body: It is fine, just commented to say something ;)

commits.message:
Extend `isIntegerInRepresentableRange` to cover large float integers

The previous implementation of `DecomposedFloat::isIntegerInRepresentableRange`
only recognized integers with normalizedExponent <= mantissa_bits (23 for
Float32, 52 for Float64). This missed all integers with higher exponents,
where all mantissa bits contribute to the integer part and no fractional
bits exist.

For Float32, this meant values like 1e9f (exponent 29) were not recognized
as integers, falling through to the slower float-to-string path instead of
the fast `itoa` path in `writeFloatTextFastPath`. This should show as a
performance improvement in the `float_formatting` performance test for Float32.

The fix adds the missing case: when exp > mantissa_bits, the value is always
an integer — we only need to check it fits in the target signed integer type
(Int32 for Float32, Int64 for Float64) via the exp < bits-1 bound.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend Float32 itoa fast path to exp 25-30 with rounding

For Float32 values with normalizedExponent > 24 (roughly 33M to 2B), the
ULP exceeds 1, so multiple integers map to the same float. A plain
Int32 cast gives the exact value, but shortest-representation algorithms
like dragonbox prefer "rounder" decimals (more trailing zeros).

This adds a rounding step that finds the roundest integer within the valid
dragonbox range, matching dragonbox output exactly for all 2.1 billion
positive Float32 values (verified exhaustively).

The rounding uses hardcoded % 10 / % 100 / % 1000 with compile-time
constant divisors (imul+shift, no hardware div). IEEE 754 boundary
tie-breaking (inclusive for even mantissa, exclusive for odd) is folded
into the half_ulp values, eliminating branches.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Clean up comments and simplify itoa dispatch in Float32 rounding path

Fix stale comments after multiple iterations: separate the general
explanation from the function documentation, correct function name
references, and clarify the exhaustive verification claim.

Simplify the itoa condition in `writeFloatTextFastPath` to use
`isIntegerInRepresentableRange` instead of manual mantissa bit masking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend itoa fast path to Float64 exp 54-62 with rounding

Same approach as the Float32 rounding: for Float64 values with
normalizedExponent 54-62 (roughly 1.8e16 to 9.2e18), find the "roundest"
integer within the valid dragonbox range using compile-time constant
modulo operations (% 10, % 100, % 1000, % 10000).

Also adds exp=53 (ULP=2) to the plain itoa path, same as Float32 exp=24.

Verified: exhaustive check of all 2.1B positive Float32 values, plus
1 billion random Float64 samples (100M per exponent) and all power-of-2
boundary values — zero mismatches with dragonbox.

Add a performance test for Float64 large integers starting at 2^53.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix rounding for negative floats, extend Float64 fast path

- Fix negative value handling in the rounding path: the modulo logic
  assumed positive values. For negative floats (e.g. BFloat16, negative
  Float64), we now write the sign first, then round the absolute value.
  Fixes 03366_bfloat16_sorting, 03367_bfloat16_tuple_final, and
  01177_group_array_moving test failures.

- Update verification comments to reflect actual testing coverage:
  Float32: all ~4.3B values exhaustively (positive and negative).
  Float64: 10B random samples (500M per exponent × 10 × pos/neg)
  plus all power-of-2 boundaries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
Add NO_INLINE to large integer fast path

Address review feedback: template, NO_INLINE, unsigned types, collapsed branch

- Replace TRY_ROUND_TO_SHORTEST macro with `tryRoundToShortest` template
  function for better type safety and debuggability.
- Add NO_INLINE to both rounding functions to prevent the compiler from
  inlining them into the hot dispatch path (was causing a 10-15% Float64
  regression from icache pressure — 1679 bytes inlined vs 284 bytes out-of-line).
- Use unsigned types (UInt32/UInt64) for half_ulp, lo, hi since they are
  always positive after sign handling.
- Collapse the two dependent branches in the pick logic into a single
  `prefer_up` expression, reducing instructions by 12-15%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add NO_INLINE to large integer fast path

Add a stateless correctness test for the extended itoa fast path covering
Float32 exp 24-30 and Float64 exp 53-62: power-of-2 boundaries, their
neighbors, negative values, and BFloat16. Fix a typo in a comment
referencing `writeFloatTextFastPathRounded` instead of
`writeFloatTextFastPathFloat32Rounded`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Fix distributed `OFFSET` returning fewer rows
body:
The following query returns `20` but should be `25`:

```sql
SELECT count() FROM (SELECT number FROM remote('127.0.0.{1,2,3}', numbers_mt(10)) OFFSET 5);
```

We only avoided applying `OFFSET` at stage 4, but we should also avoid it at stage 3. Stage 3 can be reached by the localhost shard when `createLocalPlan` downgrades stage 4 to stage 3 to preserve `rows_before_limit_at_least` correctness. As a result, localhost shard ends up applying the offset, leading to the offset being applied twice in total.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- Bug Fix (user-visible misbehavior in an official stable release)


### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
Fix `OFFSET` being applied twice in distributed queries when `prefer_localhost_replica=1`, producing fewer rows than expected.

title: Fix exception when processing duplicate aliases in nested scopes
body:
When `global_with_aliases` is copied from a parent scope to a child scope, the `QueryTreeNodePtr` smart pointers in `nodes_with_duplicated_aliases` reference the same underlying node objects. If a child scope processes a duplicate and calls `removeAlias`, the parent scope's reference to the same node will also see the empty alias, causing a logical error: "Node ... with duplicate alias does not exist in alias table"

The fix skips nodes whose aliases have already been removed by another scope's duplicate processing.

Found by BuzzHouse fuzzer.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)


See https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=95078&sha=90a42d54dafe000546a3ebd79df554a9ce97e3f8&name_0=PR&name_1=BuzzHouse%20%28amd_ubsan%29
author: alexey-milovidov

commits.message:
Fix exception when processing duplicate aliases in nested scopes

When `global_with_aliases` is copied from a parent scope to a child scope,
the `QueryTreeNodePtr` smart pointers in `nodes_with_duplicated_aliases`
reference the same underlying node objects. If a child scope processes a
duplicate and calls `removeAlias`, the parent scope's reference to the
same node will also see the empty alias, causing a logical error:
"Node ... with duplicate alias does not exist in alias table"

The fix skips nodes whose aliases have already been removed by another
scope's duplicate processing.

Found by BuzzHouse fuzzer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update test to use 3 levels of nesting to reproduce the bug

The bug requires 3 levels of nesting:
1. Outer level with WITH alias X
2. Middle level with same WITH alias X (creates duplicate in middle scope)
3. Inner level that inherits the duplicate, processes it, removes the alias

When middle scope later processes its duplicates, the node's alias is
already gone because it was removed by the inner scope's processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

title: Fix exception in assertHasValidVersionMetadata due to in-memory CSN caching
body:
## Summary

- Fix a `chassert` failure in `assertHasValidVersionMetadata` that occurs when CSN values are cached in memory but not yet written to the on-disk version metadata file
- `VersionMetadata::isVisible` legitimately stores CSN values learned from the transaction log into memory without writing them to disk. When `assertHasValidVersionMetadata` later compares in-memory values with on-disk values, it incorrectly detects a mismatch
- The specific scenario: during a merge with transactions, after the part is committed, `isVisible` or `afterCommit` sets `creation_csn` in memory. If an exception then triggers task cancellation, `removeIfNeeded` → `assertHasValidVersionMetadata` reads the on-disk file (without the CSN) and finds it doesn't match the in-memory value (with the CSN)

CI report: https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=98276&sha=2f17ecab6574a1c224f426aa57b1465978c61629&name_0=PR&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28arm_asan%2C%20s3%29

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)

### Changelog entry (a user-readable short description of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
...

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
author: alexey-milovidov

commits.message:
Fix exception in `assertHasValidVersionMetadata` due to in-memory CSN caching

`VersionMetadata::isVisible` caches `creation_csn` and `removal_csn`
in memory from the transaction log without writing them to disk.
When `assertHasValidVersionMetadata` later compares the in-memory
values with on-disk values, it finds a mismatch and throws, causing
a `chassert` failure in ASan builds.

The specific scenario: during a merge with transactions, after the
part is committed (Active state), `isVisible` or `afterCommit` sets
`creation_csn` in memory. If an exception then triggers task
cancellation, `removeIfNeeded` calls `assertHasValidVersionMetadata`
which reads the on-disk file (without the CSN) and compares it with
the in-memory value (with the CSN), causing the assertion to fail.

Allow CSN values to be absent from the on-disk file when they are
present in memory, since this is a legitimate state during the
window between CSN caching and disk write.

https://s3.amazonaws.com/clickhouse-test-reports/json.html?PR=98276&sha=2f17ecab6574a1c224f426aa57b1465978c61629&name_0=PR&name_1=Stress%20test%20%28arm_asan%2C%20s3%29

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement object-based snapshot in keeper. Closes https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/issues/95766. Added new setting `snapshot_transfer_chunk_size`, by default equal to `0`. 0 means disabled: the whole snapshot is sent as a single NuRaft object. This value should be used until all keeper servers upgrade to version supporting object based snapshots to avoid issues during upgrade.

use-case: authentication in private docker repos in integration tests

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Adds CI/integration-test authentication infrastructure so integration tests can pull images from private registries (AWS ECR), and extends Praktika to support job-level pre/post hooks with controllable execution for local runs.

**Changes:**
- Add ECR login support to the integration test cluster startup by reading an auth token JSON and performing `docker login` before image pulls.
- Extend Praktika jobs with `pre_hooks` and make hook execution configurable via a new CLI flag for local runs.
- Add a CI helper script to fetch ECR authorization tokens.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 9 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| tests/integration/helpers/cluster.py | Adds ECR login flow and passes stdin to subprocess calls for `docker login`. |
| ci/praktika/runner.py | Introduces job `pre_hooks`, changes when/where hooks are executed, and adds a `run_hooks` control flag. |
| ci/praktika/job.py | Adds `pre_hooks` to job configuration. |
| ci/praktika/__main__.py | Adds `--run-hooks-locally` and wires `run_hooks` through the CLI. |
| ci/jobs/scripts/get_ecr_token.py | New script to retrieve ECR auth tokens. |
</details>



<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1)</summary>

**ci/praktika/runner.py:576**
* Post hooks were moved out of `_post_run()` and are now executed after `_post_run()` has already written the job outputs (`env.JOB_KV_DATA`) and produced reports. This breaks the existing contract implied by the nearby comment (“run after post hooks as they might modify workflow kv data”) and will prevent post hooks from affecting outputs/metadata as before. Run job post hooks before writing job outputs (or re-read env + re-write outputs after hooks) and update the comment accordingly.

title: CI: skip stateless flaky check only when no changed, previously-failed, or relevant tests exist
body:
The stateless flaky check was previously skipped whenever no test files under `tests/queries/0_stateless` were modified in the PR. This was too aggressive: a PR that only touches C++ source code may still have previously-failed tests from earlier CI runs on the same PR, or coverage-relevant tests identified by `find_tests.py` — both are valid reasons to run the flaky check.

The new logic in `should_skip_job` uses `Targeting` to collect all three test sources before deciding to skip:

1. Changed/new test files (`get_changed_tests`) — same file-based check as before
2. Previously-failed tests from CIDB (`get_previously_failed_tests`) — tests that failed in prior CI runs for this PR
3. Coverage-relevant tests (`get_most_relevant_tests`) — tests identified by line-coverage data for the changed source files

The job is skipped only when all three return empty. Errors from CIDB queries are caught and treated as empty (fail-open), so a temporary CIDB outage cannot cause a valid flaky check to be wrongly skipped.

The integration flaky check is unchanged — it retains the original `has_new_integration_tests` file-based check, since coverage-based relevance for integration tests is not yet supported.

### Changelog category (leave one):
- CI Fix or Improvement (changelog entry is not required)

### Changelog entry (a [user-readable short description](https://github.com/ClickHouse/ClickHouse/blob/master/docs/changelog_entry_guidelines.md) of the changes that goes into CHANGELOG.md):
...

### Documentation entry for user-facing changes

- [ ] Documentation is written (mandatory for new features)
author: fm4v

commits.message:
CI: skip stateless flaky check only when no changed, previously-failed, or relevant tests exist

Previously, the stateless flaky check job was skipped whenever no test
files under `tests/queries/0_stateless` were changed in the PR. This
was too aggressive: PRs that only change C++ source code could still
have previously-failed tests from earlier CI runs, or coverage-relevant
tests found via `find_tests.py`, both of which are valid reasons to run
the flaky check.

The new logic uses `Targeting` to collect all three test sources before
deciding to skip:
1. Changed/new test files in the PR (`get_changed_tests`)
2. Previously-failed tests from CIDB (`get_previously_failed_tests`)
3. Coverage-relevant tests (`get_most_relevant_tests`)

The job is skipped only when all three return empty. Errors fetching
from CIDB are caught and treated as empty (fail-open), so a temporary
CIDB outage does not cause false skips.

The integration flaky check retains the original `has_new_integration_tests`
check, as coverage-based relevance is not yet supported for integration tests.

tests/clickhouse-test: fail test instead of silently continuing when SYSTEM SET COVERAGE TEST fails

In per-test coverage mode, a failed `SYSTEM SET COVERAGE TEST` call was
previously only printed and execution continued. This means coverage data
could accumulate under a stale test name or be dropped entirely, silently
corrupting the coverage data used by targeted test selection, while the
job still appeared green.

Now the test is marked as failed with an explanatory message if the
command cannot be armed. This makes the failure visible and prevents
building targeted test selection from incomplete data.

Addresses review comment on ClickHouse/ClickHouse#101432.

coverage: save node->count before zeroing it in getCurrentIndirectCalls

The counter was zeroed before being stored in the result, so every row
emitted by `getCurrentIndirectCalls` had `call_count = 0`, making
`system.coverage_indirect_calls.call_count` useless for weighting or
diagnostics.

Fix: capture the value into `call_count` first, then zero `node->count`.

CI: fix Targeting.job_type in filter_job — use job_name arg, not _info_cache.job_name

_info_cache is initialized from Info() once for the hook runner job, so
_info_cache.job_name is the config/hook runner name, not the flaky check
job being filtered. Targeting.__init__ derived job_type from this wrong
name, leaving job_type = None for all flaky check jobs.

Consequence: get_previously_failed_tests asserted on job_type and threw,
get_most_relevant_tests also failed, both were caught and treated as
empty, and the flaky check was incorrectly skipped whenever changed_tests
was also empty.

Fix: after constructing Targeting, override job_type from the job_name
argument passed to should_skip_job.
